Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Appearance At Depostion Of Smg Employees To Take These Depositions Up To Time Of Trial And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Eustace De Saint Phalle, for Plaintiff/Moving party William Tagoai, Nina Tagoai
Chris Strunk, for Defendant/Opposing party SMG Corporation
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: (1) Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Appearance At Deposition Of SMG Employees Jim Willers, Charlie Tumulty, Mark Wilson, Bill Jovich, Johnny Carrosio, Ronnie Lee, Brian Goodsteed, Michael Roach, Ilona Lewis, and John Plume; And To Take These Depositions Up To Time Of Trial is moot as to certain SMG employees. As to the remaining employees, who have not been deposed as of this date, their depositions shall be completed no later than May 19, 2006. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Notices Of "Persons Most Knowledgable" Of Deftsmg Moscone Center To Permit Taking Of Said Depositions Up To Time Of Trial; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Eustace De Saint Phalle, for Plaintiff/Moving party William Tagoai, Nina Tagoai
Chris Strunk, for Defendant/Opposing party SMG Corporation
Ruling:
Hearing held on (2) Plaintiffs William Tagoia, Nina Tagoia's Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Notices Of Persons Most Knowledgeable Of Defendant SMG Moscone Center; To Permit Taking Of Said Depositions Up To Time Of Trial.
Ruling after hearing: GRANTED. The PMK deposition is ordered to proceed as to topics which have not been the subject of prior testimony from employees. The deposition shall proceed up to May 26, 2006. Counsel are ordered to meet and confer as to the breadth of the deposition, narrowing the deposition to subjects that were not previously testified to by the individual employee now being designated as the PMK. As to those individuals who are designated as PMK, questions may be objected to as asked and answered if the individual gave testimony on the subject in his or her individual capacity. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

MINI MINUTES:

Eustace De Saint Phalle, Esq., of The Veen Firm, 673-4800, for Plaintiff and Opposing Party William Tagoai

Stephen Schori, Esq., of Costella & Associates, (925) 945-4491, for Defendant/Cross-Complainant/Cross-Defendant and Opposing Party McMaster-Carr Supply
